events.title: Half of world’s children exposed to at least three climate hazards, Unicef says
events.description: Almost every child, including those from high-income countries, is now exposed to at least one hazard Half of the world’s children are exposed to at least three overlapping climate hazards threatening their health, education and survival, according to a Unicef report. Globally, children face increasing threats from heatwaves, storms, floods and droughts as the climate crisis worsens, with more than one billion facing at least three of these at once. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Half of world’s children exposed to at least three climate hazards, Unicef says

events.title: Violent attacks on schools, pupils and staff around the world up by 40%, says study
events.description: Cases reported in 83 countries, with at least 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted or arrested, GCPEA says Attacks on education globally have surged by 40% with more than 8,556 recorded incidents and 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted, arrested or otherwise harmed in 2024 and 2025, according to new research. Attacks were reported in 83 countries, with the highest incidences recorded in Colombia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, Haiti, Palestine and Ukraine. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks
events.description: A Guardian analysis reveals how most of 39 countries facing US entry restrictions are most vulnerable environmentally ‘Every day it’s more barriers’: how the US is shutting out climate refugees Donald Trump ’s immigration crackdown is largely targeting people from the countries most vulnerable to displacement from climate-driven disasters, a Guardian analysis shows. As the Trump administration pushes policies to boost planet-heating fossil fuels , millions of people are being forced to flee their homelands due to storms, floods and droughts worsened by the climate crisis . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks

events.title: Hundreds still homeless one month on from South Africa's Western Cape floods
events.description: South Africa’s Western Cape is still taking stock after last month’s severe and fatal storms. 11 people were killed and tens of thousands of homes were damaged. The area is known for its wine cultivation and many of those displaced are farm workers whose accommodation on their employers’ land was completely wiped out. Mercia Andrews works with an outreach organisation that has been trying to support some of those displaced by the floods..
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Hundreds still homeless one month on from South Africa's Western Cape floods

events.title: Christian leaders hold emergency summit in Jerusalem to confront global rise in antisemitism
events.description: International Christian Embassy Jerusalem is hosting an emergency summit addressing rising antisemitism and replacement theology amid church leaders' silence.

events.title: 'A good feeling': How fans in Iran experienced World Cup opener
events.description: Iran twice came from behind to salvage a 2–2 draw against New Zealand in a World Cup opener overshadowed by geopolitical tensions, protests and months of conflict with the United States. In Tehran, the match was an opportunity for many to forget for a moment the turmoil in the country as they came together at watch parties to cheer on their team.

events.title: Sri Lanka sees ‘alarming’ rise in cybercrime as scam networks relocate from south-east Asia
events.description: Experts say criminal networks favour Sri Lanka due to ease of getting tourist visas and limited regulation on sim cards and internet connections Experts have warned that Sri Lanka is emerging as a hub for transnational cybercrime, after a crackdown in south-east Asia pushed Chinese-run criminal networks to relocate their vast scam operations. Sri Lankan police spokesperson Fredrick Wootler said the country was witnessing an “alarming increase of cybercrimes” perpetrated by people entering the country as tourists, and then illegally setting up scam operations targeting people across the world. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sri Lanka sees ‘alarming’ rise in cybercrime as scam networks relocate from south-east Asia

events.title: Two arrested during investigation into Dezi Freeman’s movements as police reveal killer likely travelled to NSW

In a statement on Tuesday, police also revealed they believed Freeman travelled between Victoria and New South Wales in the seven months between the shootings at Porepunkah and when he was found on a remote property in Thologolong, near Walwa on the Victoria-New South Wales border, on 30 March. events.title: Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds
events.description: Amazon and Sony among firms that may have sourced coltan, used in phones, from supply chains controlled by the M23 rebels, says Global Witness Leading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims. The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) that are occupied by the M23 militia , which has committed myriad atrocities in eastern DRC. events.title: Starbucks Korea to temporarily shut all stores for history lesson after bungled coffee promotion
events.description: The closures, so employees can watch a recorded lecture, will cost the company an estimated 2.1bn won ($1.4m) in sales Starbucks Korea will simultaneously close all its stores for a mandatory history lesson, after a disastrous promotion that evoked memories of a pro-democracy massacre sparked public and political backlash . More than 2,000 stores will temporarily close at 3pm on 22 June, the company said, so staff can watch recorded lectures on modern Korean history and engage in “social sensitivity” training. The half-day closures will cost Starbucks an estimated 2.1bn won ($1.4m) in lost sales, according to data firm IGAWorks. Continue reading...

events.title: Two arrested as police investigate Dezi Freeman’s movements – as it happened
events.description: This is blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Gallagher on One Nation: ‘Opinion polls will come and go’ Katy Gallagher was also asked about the surge in One Nation, saying there was an obvious “reorganisation on the conservative side of politics”. I think there is a lot of uncertainty and worry about events that are happening across the world, the impact that has here. Cost of living is the No 1 issue with anyone you talk to right now, and the government just has to remain focused on that, and that’s what we intend to do. Opinion polls will come and go, they do. Our job is to make sure we’re making the right decisions for the right time and that it’s focused on the Australian people. And when there’s controversy about that, and we know we’ve had a lot of feedback around the budget, you know, our job is to front up and explain why we’ve taken those decisions. Continue reading...

events.description: Reserve Bank of Australia decision on Tuesday comes after three consecutive cash rate hikes this year Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Reserve Bank has warned it is ready to raise interest rates further despite leaving its official interest rate on hold at 4.35%. The widely expected decision on Tuesday will bring little relief to mortgage holders, already strained by the RBA’s three consecutive rate hikes earlier in 2026. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 RBA interest rates: Reserve Bank holds official cash rate at 4.35%

events.title: Trump announces deal to end war in Iran. And, how to stay safe in deadly heat
events.description: Trump says a deal has been reached to end the war between the U.S. and Iran. Plus, this is what extreme temperatures do to the human body — and how you can keep yourself safe.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump announces deal to end war in Iran. And, how to stay safe in deadly heat

events.title: US-Iran deal: Tehran says Israel withdrawal from Lebanon key
events.description: Iran's top diplomat says any continued Israeli presence in Lebanon would violate the interim US–Iran deal to end the war. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu insists Israel will remain as long as necessary. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran deal: Tehran says Israel withdrawal from Lebanon key

events.title: French startup unveils non-humanoid robot as AI race moves to physical machines
events.description: French robotics startup Genesis AI on Tuesday unveiled "Eno", its first general-purpose robot, marking a step toward bringing advanced AI from online chatbots into physical machines. Backed by former Google CEO Eric Schmidt, the company says the wheeled robot is designed to extend human capabilities rather than mimic human form, with commercial deployments planned from late 2026.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 French startup unveils non-humanoid robot as AI race moves to physical machines

events.title: Trump says Russia 'should make a deal' after G7 meeting with Zelensky
events.description: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday that "Russia should make a deal" to end its war against Ukraine, after meeting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the G7 summit in France. France 24's Philip Turle has the latest from the meeting in Évian-les-Bains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump says Russia 'should make a deal' after G7 meeting with Zelensky

events.title: No toll but ‘maritime service fees’ for Strait of Hormuz?
events.description: Shipping remains virtually at a standstill through the Strait of Hormuz, according to tracking platforms. That's despite US President Donald Trump's claim that crossings were resuming under his deal to end the war with Iran. Meanwhile a row is emerging over Iran's plan to charge levies on shipping.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 No toll but ‘maritime service fees’ for Strait of Hormuz?

events.title: Iran deal is tacit admission of strategic defeat by Trump administration
events.description: The Iran "deal" is a tacit admission of strategic defeat by the Trump administration and of a failure to achieve nearly all of his war aims.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran deal is tacit admission of strategic defeat by Trump administration

events.title: Brazilian police say woman who rope jumped to her death was not tied by instructors
events.description: A Brazilian police investigator says that a 21-year-old woman who died in a rope jumping incident over the weekend was not attached to any safety equipment

events.title: Owners of Dominican club where 236 died in collapse to stand trial

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies
events.description: Outpouring of public grief for Lidia ‘Taty’ Almeida, leader of group of mothers that has marched every week since 1977 The human rights activist Lidia “Taty” Almeida – who spent more than half a century searching for her son after he was forcibly disappeared by Argentina’s military junta – has died aged 95, prompting a public outpouring of grief. Almeida, 95, was the president of the Mothers of Plaza de Mayo, made up of women who have marched around the square outside Argentina’s presidential palace every Thursday since 1977 , demanding the return of children who were disappeared during the country’s 1976-1983 dictatorship. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies

events.title: France braces for another heatwave as temperatures soar
events.description: France is bracing for another heatwave with temperatures set to soar to 40 degrees celsius in parts of the country in the coming days. This heatwave is promising to be even more challenging than the last one in May.

events.title: Eight dead after US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes in California
events.description: The incident occurred on Monday morning while the aircraft had been on a routine test mission.

events.title: California's Gavin Newsom alleges justice department is investigating his wife and ex-staff

events.description: Analysts say Britain is responding to civil disobedience with unprecedented severity.

events.title: Russian artist critical of Putin shot dead in Poland
events.description: Three days before his killing, Semyon Skrepetsky had protested outside the Russian embassy in Berlin. He held an icon-like caricature of Russian President Vladimir Putin with Soviet leader Josef Stalin.

events.title: Homes in illegal Israeli settlements promoted at London property show
events.description: Sky News has obtained exclusive images that demonstrate illegal Israeli settlements in the West Bank were being advertised at a controversial property show that attracted large protests in London on Sunday.

events.title: Rwanda opposition leader Ingabire says she's physically unfit for trial

events.title: UNRWA fires 70 Gaza staffers amid allegations of Hamas ties, says terminations not admission of guilt

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Woman, 21, dies after being thrown from Brazil rope jump bridge without harness
events.description: Instructors hurled Maria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas into 40-metre abyss without attaching safety equipment A 21-year-old woman who died when two rope-jumping instructors threw her from a bridge without first harnessing her to security equipment has been buried in Brazil ’s São Paulo state. Maria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas was rope jumping on Saturday at Ponte do Esqueleto, an abandoned bridge in the municipality of Limeira where tourists practise extreme sports. The young woman, who aspired to become a physical education teacher, had asked to be launched from the bridge airplane-style, with two instructors hoisting her above their shoulders as she spread out her arms. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Woman, 21, dies after being thrown from Brazil rope jump bridge without harness

events.title: European Parliament approves US trade deal to lower tariffs
events.description: The European Parliament has approved cutting tariffs on many US imports to comply with a deal signed last year. US President Trump had given the bloc a July 4 deadline to meet its side of the deal.

events.title: Authorities say at least 9 dead, 25 hurt after a train and bus collide in Zimbabwe
events.description: Police and rail authorities say at least nine people, including two children, are dead after a train collided with a bus in Zimbabwe

events.title: Mike Waltz says Gulf allies back Trump’s Iran pressure campaign after regional trip: ‘Zero daylight’
events.description: Ambassador Mike Waltz says Gulf allies back Trump's blockade and economic pressure campaign against Iran, with UAE and Bahrain validating U.S. strategy.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Musk to sue German broadcaster ZDF over 'hunt for migrants'
events.description: Elon Musk said he was taking legal action against one of Germany's public broadcasters after it reported that he had called for "a hunt for migrants" in Belfast.

events.title: 'The case of Lyhanna is not exceptional': Head of Women for Women France
events.description: The CEO of a French group that fights for the rights of women and children in France has hit out at the French government and justice system. Her comments come after the murder of 11-year-old Lyhanna. The prime suspect in the case is a man who had previously been reported to police as a suspected sex offender. Sarah McGrath of Women for Women France says the case of Lyhanna is unfortunately not exceptional, and shows the typical functioning of the French justice system. She spoke to us in Perspective.

events.title: Mass rollout planned across Southern Africa for new HIV drug
events.description: Administered just twice a year, the drug Lenacapavir offers near-complete protection — more than 99% — against HIV. After years of research and clinical trials, the preventive treatment has been approved and is now set to be rolled out on a large scale. Mass rollout campaigns are planned across Southern Africa — South Africa, Zambia, Eswatini, Zimbabwe, Lesotho and Mozambique — the region hardest hit by HIV/AIDS. Our team on the ground reports.

events.title: Sweden requires public workers to report migrants not authorized to live there
events.description: Sweden's parliament has passed a law requiring public sector workers to report unauthorized migrants to the police

events.title: Ivy League student investigated after alleged antisemitic internship rejection: 'Not working for a Jew'
events.description: Cornell investigates after student Austin Franco reportedly rejected a Jewish-owned startup internship, saying he's not interested in working for a Jew.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Three children shot near public pool in small Arkansas town, suspect taken into custody

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly

events.title: Canada eliminates human rights watchdog that oversees companies operating abroad
events.description: Mark Carney says Canadian Ombudsperson for Responsible Enterprise office hasn’t been ‘effective’ since its 2019 setup Canada is eliminating a watchdog that investigates alleged human rights violations committed by Canadian companies operating abroad, after Mark Carney said the office hadn’t been “effective” since it was set up in 2019. The move comes as Canada faces criticism from Donald Trump’s administration over its “unacceptable” efforts to combat forced labour. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump arrives in France for G7 leaders summit, touting peace deal with Iran

events.title: Crude oil is cheaper as markets embrace news of a U.S.-Iran deal
events.description: Crude oil prices are falling as oil markets react to President Trump saying the U.S. and Iran have agreed on a framework for reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Germany: 25 years of compensating Nazi-era forced laborers
events.description: A German fund set up to compensate the millions forced to work for the Nazi regime is marking 25 years since the first payments were made. But for many victims, it was much too late.

events.title: How Germany and Poland rebooted relations 35 years ago
events.description: When Germany and Poland signed the Treaty on Good Neighborliness, Friendship and Cooperation in June 1991, they chose partnership over enmity and became close NATO and EU allies. But it hasn't all been plain sailing.

events.title: Diplomats and abuse — chipping at the shied of immunity
events.description: Domestic workers who were mistreated by diplomats are often denied justice due to their abusers enjoying diplomatic immunity. But court rulings in the UK and Switzerland have paved the way for change.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Diplomats and abuse — chipping at the shied of immunity

events.title: Germany news: Population shrinks for first time since COVID-induced dip in 2020
events.description: A dip in net migration meant it could not cancel out Germany's low birth rates in 2025. Meanwhile, fuel prices fell amid news of a possible deal between the US and Iran. And Merz gives Trump a Germany jersey. More on DW.

events.title: Sigh of relief in Africa as the Strait of Hormuz 'reopens'
events.description: A potential US-Iran agreement could lower energy-, fertilizer- and food prices in Africa if the Strait of Hormuz fully reopens. Oil exporters such as Nigeria and Angola might have to cope with less revenue, experts say.

events.title: Charging ahead: EVs outpace growth predictions
events.description: Electric cars are getting cheaper, more efficient and can travel farther than ever. China is driving the transition, but Europe and other countries are catching up fast.

events.title: Artist JR's wrapped bridge opens in Paris in nod to Christo
events.description: After a weather-delayed start, the French street artist's cavernous optical illusion takes over one of the most iconic bridges in Paris. It's a tribute to Christo and Jeanne-Claude's famous "Pont Neuf Wrapped."

events.title: Eldest son of Norway’s crown princess sentenced to 4 years in prison for rape
events.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the eldest son of Norway’s Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was sentenced to four years in prison Monday after being convicted of rape. He was found guilty of two of the four rape charges he was facing and acquitted of the other two.

events.title: Stepson of Norway's crown prince jailed for two rapes
events.description: The stepson of Norway's Crown Prince Haakon has been jailed for two rapes - including one in the basement of his family's home.

events.title: Swedish court imprisons man convicted of pressuring his wife to sell sexual services

events.title: German president says Europe is worried over tensions in the disputed South China Sea

events.title: Syrian sentenced to 26 years in prison by Dutch court for crimes against humanity

events.title: Zelenskyy congratulates Trump on turning 80 in birthday call, vows more Ukraine peace talks at G7 on Tuesday
events.description: Zelenskyy congratulated Trump on turning 80 and discussed peace efforts in a phone call ahead of the G7 Summit, where the two leaders agreed to meet.

events.title: Germany pledges to build Europe's strongest army as NATO allies answer Trump pressure

events.title: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone flights
events.description: South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years for allegedly ordering drone flights over North Korea to justify martial law.
